# Estimating Significant Left Turns: Lisbon to Singapore

## Route Overview

The shortest road route Lisbon → Spain → France/Balkans → Turkey → Iran → Pakistan → India → Myanmar → Thailand → Malaysia → Singapore covers approximately **15,000 km**.

## Segment-by-Segment Estimation

| Segment | Distance | Terrain/Road Type | Left turns/km | Left Turns |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Iberia (highways) | 1,400 km | Flat motorway | 0.03 | ~42 |
| Southern Europe → Turkey | 2,500 km | Mix highway/mountain | 0.05 | ~125 |
| Western Turkey | 1,000 km | Good roads | 0.10 | ~100 |
| Eastern Turkey (mountains) | 500 km | Winding mountain | 0.50 | ~250 |
| Iran | 2,000 km | Decent roads, some desert | 0.15 | ~300 |
| Pakistan | 1,500 km | Balochistan hills, mixed | 0.30 | ~450 |
| India | 3,000 km | Congested, many towns | 0.25 | ~750 |
| Myanmar | 1,000 km | Very mountainous, winding | 0.80 | ~800 |
| Thailand → Malaysia → Singapore | 2,000 km | Good highways | 0.10 | ~200 |
| **City passages** (~60 cities, ~3 left turns each) | — | Urban navigation | — | ~180 |

## Key Reasoning

- **Highways** contribute very few sharp turns (gentle curves rarely exceed 45°)
- **Mountain roads** (E. Turkey, Myanmar) have switchbacks with frequent >45° bends — roughly half are leftward
- **Asian intercity roads** pass through many towns requiring intersection turns
- Left/right turns are ~50/50 over long distances (no systematic directional bias on a roughly eastward route)

## Final Estimate

**≈ 3,200 significant left turns**

# Estimating Left Turns: Lisbon to Singapore

## Route Overview

The shortest road route (~15,000 km) would go roughly:
**Lisbon → Spain → France → Turkey → Iran → Pakistan → India → Myanmar → Thailand → Malaysia → Singapore**

## Breaking the Route into Segments by Road Type

| Segment | Distance | Type | Total sharp turns/km | Total turns |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Lisbon → Istanbul (European motorways) | ~4,500 km | Highway | ~0.03 | ~135 |
| Istanbul → Tehran (E. Turkey mountains, Iranian highways) | ~2,500 km | Mixed/mountain | ~0.06 | ~150 |
| Tehran → Lahore (desert + Balochistan passes) | ~3,000 km | Mixed | ~0.05 | ~150 |
| Lahore → Myanmar border (Indian national highways, towns) | ~2,500 km | Busy mixed | ~0.08 | ~200 |
| Myanmar (Shan highlands, winding mountain roads) | ~1,500 km | Mountain | ~0.25 | ~375 |
| Thailand → Singapore (good highways) | ~1,500 km | Highway | ~0.03 | ~45 |

- **Total significant turns (>45°, both directions):** ~1,055 + ~250 additional urban navigation turns through ~35 major cities ≈ **1,300 total turns**
- **Left vs. right split:** The route trends eastward with no systematic directional bias, so roughly **50% are left turns**
- **Estimated left turns:** 1,300 × 0.5 ≈ **650**

## Key Considerations
- Mountain switchbacks (Myanmar, eastern Turkey, Balochistan) dominate the count despite being a fraction of the distance
- European/Malaysian motorways contribute very few turns per km
- 45° is a high threshold — gentle highway curves don't qualify, but switchbacks (120–180°) absolutely do

## **My estimate: 650**

$$\boxed{1{,}900}$$

## Route Overview

| Road Type | % of Route | Distance | Left turns/km | Left Turns |
|---|---|---|---|---|

| Gently curving highway | 30% | 4,500 km | 0.05 | ~225 |
| Curving road (hilly, following rivers) | 17% | 2,550 km | 0.20 | ~510 |
| Mountain road (E. Turkey, Iran, Myanmar) | 15% | 2,250 km | 0.65 | ~1,460 |
| Switchback mountain passes | 3% | 450 km | 1.50 | ~675 |

## Final Estimate

$$\boxed{3{,}500}$$

|---|---|---|---|

| Urban navigation (~35 major cities) | 1,000 | 0.30 | 300 |

## My Estimate

## **3,500**

## Establishing the Route

- **Mountain roads dominate the count.** Switchbacks alternate left/right every 200–400m; moderate winding roads yield a left curve every ~700m.
